content: Climate change affects health through multiple pathways: (1) Direct effects - heat-related illness and mortality (heat waves cause 5,000-10,000 excess deaths annually in Europe alone), extreme weather injuries; (2) Vector-borne diseases - expanded geographic range of mosquitoes transmitting malaria, dengue, Zika (dengue cases increased 8x globally since 2000); (3) Food security - crop yield reductions affecting nutrition (projected 20% increase in malnutrition by 2050); (4) Air quality - increased ozone and particulate matter from wildfires exacerbating respiratory/cardiovascular disease; (5) Water-borne diseases - flooding contaminating water supplies; (6) Mental health - climate anxiety, displacement trauma. WHO estimates climate change causes 150,000 deaths annually currently, projected to reach 250,000/year by 2030-2050.
